discoveredIssues.summary: 175 working-tree files modified outside spec 095's planning surface (pre-existing operator WIP from parallel work, not owned by 095).
discoveredIssues.rationale: Spec 095's stage-3 full-delivery run authored its OWN diffs under the declared deliverableFiles[] (internal/retrieval/**, internal/config/retrieval.go + config.go wiring, the additive internal/pipeline/AssignTierWithEvergreen seam, the e2e/stress scripts, and the two docs). The ~175 OTHER modified files (cmd/, internal/ unrelated to retrieval, ml/, config/prometheus/, scripts/, smackerel.sh, plus unrelated specs/*) are pre-existing operator working-tree changes from parallel spec work that predate this run and are DISJOINT from spec 095's surface. They are NOT spec 095 deliverables and MUST NOT be interpreted as 095 evidence. The state-transition-guard Check 3B (Gate G073) scans the WHOLE working tree and flags these foreign files as undeclared edits relative to deliverableFiles[] — this whole-tree conflation is the operator-owned environment concern; spec 095's own diff is clean and scoped to deliverableFiles[].
discoveredIssues.action: Operator owns the disposition of the 175 WIP files (commit to their owning specs, stash, or discard). No spec 095 action required; spec 095 authored none of them.

discoveredIssues.summary: internal/deploy::TestMonitoringDocsContract_LiveFile fails on pre-existing foreign WIP — config/prometheus/alerts.yml (M) adds two alerts (SmackerelDigestSynthesisDegraded, SmackerelIntelligenceAlertProductionFailing) without the matching docs/Operations.md (M) runbook rows.

The monitoring-docs contract test asserts every alert in alerts.yml is mentioned in Operations.md; the foreign alert additions broke that contract. Editing either file to 'fix' it would mutate foreign WIP in a shared multi-agent repo (forbidden). BROADENED (closeout 2026-06-18): since the assurance increment the foreign unit-failure set grew from 1 to 3 packages — internal/config::TestSecretKeysMirror (foreign LLM_PROVIDER_SECRET_MASTER_KEY added to SecretKeys() without updating secret_keys_test.go; spec 095 never touched secret_keys*), internal/deploy (TestBundleSecretContract + this TestMonitoringDocsContract), and internal/scopesdriftguard::TestScopesPathRefDrift (global broken-ref ratchet 286>270, foreign specs 059/063/038/058). All three are foreign-WIP, none in spec 095's diff; spec-095's own tests are GREEN in isolation (report.md#closeout-2026-06-18).
discoveredIssues.action: Operator / the parallel session that authored the alert change owns adding the two runbook rows to docs/Operations.md. No spec 095 action.

discoveredIssues.summary: The evergreen seam is sited at the AssignTier 'ingestion front door', but pipeline.AssignTier (capital) has NO live production caller: PublishRawArtifact resolves tier via resolveTierFromMetadata(metadata), and connectors carry their own package-private assignTier. The delivered additive AssignTierWithEvergreen seam is correct + unit-proven (byte-for-byte AssignTier; NFR-3) but plugs into a non-live abstraction, so on its own it never scores a real ingested artifact.
discoveredIssues.rationale: Discovered by bubbles.gaps 2026-06-18 via grep of 'AssignTier(' across internal/**/*.go: the only callers are pipeline tests + the 095 seam. Not a defect in 095's package (the seam honors NFR-3); a routing correction for the PKT-095-B wiring owner so the evergreen Scorer is actually invoked at ingestion.
discoveredIssues.action: RESOLVED (Increment 2, 2026-06-18): PKT-095-B delivered. PublishRawArtifact (the real front door) now ADDITIVELY scores + persists the evergreen signal via the injected nil-safe Scorer; resolveTierFromMetadata + the tier outcome are byte-for-byte unchanged (NFR-3). AssignTierWithEvergreen is honestly re-documented as superseded (no production caller; retained as the AssignTier-unchanged unit proof). Persisted on the EXISTING artifacts table via migration 060 (Principle 5). Evidence: report.md#increment-2 (ingest_evergreen_test.go GREEN; internal/pipeline regression GREEN; TestMigrationsEmbed GREEN; check/lint EXIT 0, scenario-lint 17/0).

discoveredIssues.summary: The spec-058 Chrome-extension ingest path (extensioningest.NewHandler) constructs its OWN pipeline.RawArtifactPublisher in cmd/core/wiring.go (~L523) and does NOT yet share the evergreen Scorer, so extension-ingested artifacts persist a NULL evergreen_score until this is wired.
discoveredIssues.rationale: Increment 2 (2026-06-18) wired the evergreen Scorer into the PRIMARY front door (the connector supervisor's publisher in cmd/core/services.go) — the canonical ingestion path for all connectors. The secondary extension-ingest publisher lives in cmd/core/wiring.go, which is foreign-dirty operator WIP (DISC-095-WT-01 — an unrelated 4-line hunk at L315-327). Editing it to inject svc.evergreenScorer would entangle this increment with foreign WIP (forbidden in this shared multi-agent repo). NFR-3-safe: a NULL evergreen_score is treated as evergreen/not-excluded downstream (Principle 9), so extension artifacts are never wrongly excluded — they are simply not yet scored.
discoveredIssues.action: A later increment sets extPub.Scorer = svc.evergreenScorer in cmd/core/wiring.go (a one-line additive change, svc.evergreenScorer already available at that construction point) once the foreign cmd/core/wiring.go WIP clears. Low priority — the primary connector front door is wired + unit-proven.
discoveredIssues.owner: a later bubbles.goal increment (after cmd/core/wiring.go foreign WIP clears)

specDependsOnNote: spec 061/064/068 are listed as specIntegratesWith (not hard deps): planning-ceiling promotion does NOT require integration-target implementation completion.
